title: NOC 92021 – Supervisors, electronics and electrical products manufacturing | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors under NOC 92021 are the technical leaders of Canada's high-tech manufacturing sector. As a TEER 2 occupation, these professionals oversee the assembly, fabrication, and testing of essential electronic components and electrical apparatus—ranging from smartphone circuits and medical devices to industrial transformers and EV batteries. In 2026, with the global semiconductor market projected to exceed $1 trillion and Canada’s "Smart Manufacturing" transition in full swing, these supervisors are critical for maintaining the country’s competitive edge in innovation. For immigration, this role is a top candidate for Express Entry Category-Based Selection (STEM and Trades) and the New 2026 TR to PR Pathway.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Level2Major Group92 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsSub-Major Group920 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsMinor Group9202 – Supervisors, assembly and fabricationNOC Code92021

common_job_titles: Final Assembly and Testing Supervisor - Electronic Equipment
duties: Supervision: Coordinate and schedule the activities of workers who assemble, fabricate, inspect, and test electronic and electrical assemblies.
Operational Strategy: Establish methods to meet strict work schedules and coordinate activities with engineering and logistics departments.
Quality Control: Resolve technical work problems and recommend measures to improve productivity and product reliability.
Resource Management: Requisition materials and supplies; manage equipment maintenance and tool calibration.
Staff Development: Train workers in job duties, safety procedures, and company policies; recommend personnel actions such as hirings and promotions.
Compliance: Ensure strict adherence to safety rules, environmental regulations, and ISO quality standards.
Reporting: Prepare detailed production logs, safety audit reports, and test result summaries for plant management.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is mandatory.
Education: Post-secondary education in electronics, electrical, or manufacturing technology is usually required.
Work Experience: Several years of experience as an assembler, fabricator, inspector, or lead hand in electronics or electrical manufacturing are mandatory.
Technical Skills: Proficiency in Lean Manufacturing, PCB/PCBA assembly, and Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES).
Strong leadership, attention to detail, and the ability to manage complex, multi-stage production cycles are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, total annual earnings for senior supervisors in high-tech sectors like Semiconductors or Medical Electronics often exceed $95,000 - $125,000 inclusive of bonuses.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the Canadian consumer electronics and industrial machinery markets will grow at a CAGR of 5.7% through 2033. The primary driver is the "Digital Transformation" of the economy, which requires a constant supply of hardware. As 20% of the current supervisory workforce reaches retirement age by 2027, the "Leadership Gap" offers significant opportunities for experienced international supervisors.
labour_market_demand: The demand for electronics manufacturing supervisors is Very Good, specifically in Canada’s technology hubs. In 2026, Ontario (Toronto/Ottawa) and British Columbia (Vancouver) are reporting a high need for supervisors to manage the production of telecommunications equipment and green energy storage systems. According to recent 2026 outlooks, over 80% of manufacturers are investing 20% or more of their budgets into Smart Manufacturing, creating an urgent need for supervisors who can bridge the gap between traditional assembly and AI-integrated production.
High-Tech Innovation Hub: Work at the center of Canada's growing semiconductor and EV battery supply chains.
Technological Advancement: Manage "Industry 4.0" facilities utilizing Agentic AI, collaborative robots (cobots), and IoT-enabled quality control.
Premium Compensation: This TEER 2 role offers some of the highest wages in the manufacturing sector, with 97.5% of workers receiving comprehensive non-wage benefits.
Strategic PR Pathways: This occupation is a high priority for Express Entry Category-Based Selection for both "STEM" and "Trade" occupations.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): High priority for both the STEM and Trades categories, provided you have at least 12 months of continuous experience.
New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A temporary measure launching in April 2026 targeting 33,000 skilled workers already in Canada.
OINP Employer Job Offer (Foreign Worker Stream): A direct route for those with a job offer from an Ontario-based electronics or electrical manufacturer.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Priority for supervisors supporting the province’s high-growth clean-tech and electronics sectors.
